methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $810M - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

private.name: New Limit
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $810M

data.narrative:
DNA Script is a Series C-stage biotech company that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline, applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier for Series C advancement, yielding $340M. Peer-median analysis across 4 same-stage, same-sector private comparables—Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B), and New Limit ($810M)—produces a median valuation of $1.9B. Public-comp anchoring was not applied due to absence of disclosed revenue figures, though reference comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) show a sector P/S median of 6.1x. The disparity between stage-progression and peer-median reflects early-stage capital intensity versus market benchmarking for proven-stage peers.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $289M to $2.2B, with a median estimate of $1.1B. Key uncertainties include the extent to which peer valuations reflect comparable technology maturity and commercialization progress, and whether DNA Script's capital deployment trajectory aligns with the peer cohort. The wide range reflects genuine valuation volatility at Series C in biotech, where commercial validation remains incomplete.
